name: CI/CD MobilityKafka

on:
  push:
    branches: [ "**" ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, dev ]

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: ${{ github.repository }}

jobs:

  # ── Job 1 : Quick Java compilation check (no Docker) ──────────────────────
  compile:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Java 21
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '21'
          distribution: 'corretto'

      - name: Cache Maven
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.m2
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-maven-${{ hashFiles('**/pom.xml') }}

      - name: Install JMEOS stub in local Maven repo
        run: |
          mvn install:install-file \
            -Dfile=jar/JMEOS.jar \
            -DgroupId=org.jmeos \
            -DartifactId=jmeos \
            -Dversion=1.0-SNAPSHOT \
            -Dpackaging=jar

      - name: Compile (skip tests)
        run: mvn compile -DskipTests

  # ── Job 2 : Build Docker image ONCE (all non-Q8 classes compiled in) ───────
  #
  # Key insight: pom.xml excludes only Q8 from compilation. All other
  # Query*_Main classes land in the same fat JAR regardless of queryMainClass.
  # A single image can serve Q1–Q7 and Q9 — only the CMD needs to change.
  #
  build-base:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: compile
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set lowercase image name
        id: image
        run: echo "name=$(echo '${{ github.repository }}' | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Build image (all non-Q8 classes baked in)
        run: docker build -f Dockerfile -t query-app:ci .

      # Push versioned tags to GitHub Container Registry on main only
      - name: Push to GitHub Container Registry
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          docker tag query-app:ci ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ steps.image.outputs.name }}:latest
          docker tag query-app:ci ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ steps.image.outputs.name }}:${{ github.sha }}
          docker push ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ steps.image.outputs.name }}:latest
          docker push ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ steps.image.outputs.name }}:${{ github.sha }}

      # Share the image with test jobs via artifact (avoids rebuilding per job)
      - name: Export image as artifact
        run: docker save query-app:ci | gzip > /tmp/query-app.tar.gz

      - name: Upload image artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: query-app-image
          path: /tmp/query-app.tar.gz
          retention-days: 1  # only needed within the same workflow run

  # ── Job 3 : Integration tests Q1–Q7, Q9 (reuse single pre-built image) ────
  #
  # Each matrix job:
  #   1. Downloads the shared image artifact (built once above)
  #   2. Appends a single CMD layer pointing to the right main class — instant,
  #      no Maven, no MEOS recompilation, no full Docker rebuild
  #   3. Runs docker compose and waits for output
  #
  integration-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-base

    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - { query_key: ais1, class: AISData_Queries.Query1_Main, dataset: input_ais, alert: "[Q1]" }
          - { query_key: ais2, class: AISData_Queries.Query2_Main, dataset: input_ais, alert: "[Q2]" }
          - { query_key: ais3, class: AISData_Queries.Query3_Main, dataset: input_ais, alert: "[Q3]" }
          - { query_key: ais4, class: AISData_Queries.Query4_Main, dataset: input_ais, alert: "[Q4]" }
          - { query_key: ais5, class: AISData_Queries.Query5_Main, dataset: input_ais, alert: "[Q5]" }
          - { query_key: ais6, class: AISData_Queries.Query6_Main, dataset: input_ais, alert: "[Q6]" }
          - { query_key: ais7, class: AISData_Queries.Query7_Main, dataset: input_ais, alert: "[Q7]" }
          - { query_key: ais9, class: AISData_Queries.Query9_Main, dataset: input_ais, alert: "[Q9]" }
      fail-fast: false

    name: "Integration test — ${{ matrix.query_key }}"

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Download pre-built image
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: query-app-image
          path: /tmp

      - name: Load image into Docker daemon
        run: docker load < /tmp/query-app.tar.gz

      # Create a near-zero-cost image layer that just overrides CMD.
      # All classes are already compiled — this step takes ~1 second.
      - name: Override CMD for ${{ matrix.class }}
        run: |
          printf 'FROM query-app:ci\nCMD ["java", "--add-opens", "java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED", "-Djava.library.path=/usr/local/lib", "-cp", "target/MobilityKafka-1.0-SNAPSHOT-jar-with-dependencies.jar:/tmp/JMEOS.jar", "%s"]\n' \
            "${{ matrix.class }}" > /tmp/Dockerfile.cmd
          docker build -f /tmp/Dockerfile.cmd -t query-app .

      - name: Start stack (${{ matrix.query_key }})
        env:
          DATASET: ${{ matrix.dataset }}
        run: docker compose up -d
        timeout-minutes: 5

      - name: Wait for output
        run: |
          docker compose wait producer
          echo "Waiting for Kafka Streams output (${{ matrix.alert }})..."
          for i in $(seq 1 12); do
            OUTPUT=$(docker compose logs consumer 2>&1)
            if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qF "${{ matrix.alert }}"; then
              echo "✅ Output detected at attempt $i"
              exit 0
            fi
            echo "Attempt $i/12 — not yet, waiting 5s..."
            sleep 5
          done
          echo "❌ No output after 60s"
          docker compose logs query-app
          exit 1

      - name: Print consumer output
        if: always()
        run: docker compose logs consumer

      - name: Teardown
        if: always()
        run: docker compose down -v
